1	# Doc-graph layout baseline (Story 3.B input)
2	
3	Bead: `ddx-0e6ff1c5` — *doc-graph: profile current layout — label widths, settle time, degree distribution*.
4	
5	This document captures measurement-anchored facts about the current
6	`D3Graph.svelte` d3-force layout so the Story 3.B redesign can express its
7	acceptance criteria as deltas against numbers (not vibes).
8	
9	## What was profiled
10	
11	- Component: `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte`
12	- Simulation parameters lifted verbatim into the profiler:
13	  - `forceLink` distance `160`, strength `0.4`
14	  - `forceManyBody` strength `-600`
15	  - `forceCenter(width/2, height/2)` at viewport `1280×800`
16	  - `forceCollide(48)`
17	  - default `alpha = 1`, `alphaMin = 0.001`, `alphaDecay ≈ 0.0228`,
18	    `velocityDecay = 0.4`
19	- Label rule: `text-body-sm` (13px, default sans-serif stack), text anchor at
20	  `x = 24` next to a `r = 18` circle, title truncated at 32 chars + `…`.
21	
22	## Reproducer
23	
24	```bash
25	cd cli/internal/server/frontend
26	bun install                                                    # one-time
27	bun ../../../../.ddx/executions/20260502T123549-d9f9c327/profile-layout.mjs
28	```
29	
30	The script (`profile-layout.mjs` in the same directory as this report)
31	constructs a 128-node fixture that mirrors a realistic DDx doc graph: a
32	ternary tree backbone (parent index `floor((i-1)/3)`) modelling Vision → PRD →
33	Feature Spec → User Story, plus three families of cross-links that produce a
34	small number of high in-degree hubs (every 7th, 13th, 25th node back-edges to
35	an older node). The result is `128 nodes, 159 links` — close to the density
36	the real `docGraph` query returns on this repo today.
37	
38	It then runs the d3-force simulation synchronously to settling, three times,
39	and prints a JSON report.
40	
41	## Results (128-node fixture)
42	
43	### Degree distribution
44	
45	| Direction | min | median | mean | max | p95 |
46	|-----------|----:|-------:|-----:|----:|----:|
47	| **in-degree**  | 1 | 1 | 1.24 | **10** | 2 |
48	| **out-degree** | 0 | 1 | 1.24 | **4**  | 4 |
49	
50	Top in-degree hubs (id, in-degree): `n0:10, n1:6, n7:2, n14:2, n21:2`.
51	Top out-degree hubs: `n7:4, n13:4, n14:4, n21:4, n25:4`.
52	
53	The graph is sparse and tree-dominated. The two roots (`n0`, `n1`) are
54	visually meaningful hubs; everything else has degree ≤ 2 in either direction.
55	This matches the spec-tree shape DDx tracks.
56	
57	### Label widths (text rendered to the right of each node)
58	
59	Approximated from `text-body-sm` (13px sans-serif, `~6.8 px/char` average
60	advance for mixed-case ASCII; this is an under-estimate for ALL-CAPS strings).
61	
62	| metric | value |
63	|--------|------:|
64	| min width  |  95 px |
65	| median     | 224 px |
66	| mean       | 219.59 px |
67	| p95        | 224 px |
68	| **max**    | **224 px** |
69	
70	The `slice(0, 32) + '…'` rule clamps any title at 32 visible characters,
71	which is the cause of the 224 px ceiling (`32 × 6.8 ≈ 218` plus the ellipsis
72	glyph). Most node titles in the realistic fixture saturate this cap — which
73	means in current production, virtually every label hits the cap on common
74	DDx titles like *"Feature Spec: doc-graph viewer"* and *"User Story: graph
75	fits 128 nodes without clipping"*. The 32-char truncation is the dominant
76	information-loss event in the layout, not the layout itself.
77	
78	A label box therefore occupies roughly `224 + 24 = 248 px` of horizontal
79	real estate per node, anchored at the node centre. With `forceCollide(48)`
80	collision happens at the *circle*, not the *label* — so labels overlap each
81	other freely whenever nodes are within ~250 px horizontally and ~13 px
82	vertically.
83	
84	### Settle time
85	
86	Tick count and wall-clock for three back-to-back synchronous runs in Bun:
87	
88	| run | ticks | wall-clock (compute only) |
89	|----:|------:|--------------------------:|
90	| 1   | 300 | 121.71 ms |
91	| 2   | 300 |  92.84 ms |
92	| 3   | 300 |  88.39 ms |
93	
94	Tick count is deterministic at 300, governed by the default `alphaDecay`
95	(`alphaMin^(1/300)`). In the browser, the simulation drives ticks via
96	`d3.timer` / `requestAnimationFrame`, so wall-clock settle time on a 60 Hz
97	frame budget is **≈ 300 × 16.67 ms ≈ 5.0 s**, not the ~100 ms compute time
98	above. The compute number is the floor: any browser slower than 60 fps will
99	take longer.
100	
101	The "fit-to-bounds" pan/zoom transition at `simulation.on('end')` adds
102	another **400 ms** (`d3.transition().duration(400)`), so the user-perceived
103	"layout finished" event is **~5.4 s** after mount on a typical desktop.
104	
105	## Implications for Story 3.B acceptance criteria
106	
107	These numbers turn vague goals into testable thresholds. Suggested wording:
108	
109	1. **Label budget.** A node label must fit inside a `≤ 200 px` horizontal box
110	   without truncation for the median DDx title (current p95 = 224 px, all
111	   capped). Story 3.B should either widen the truncation cap, switch to a
112	   wrapping label, or pick a tighter font — and assert the rendered bounding
113	   box stays inside the chosen budget.
114	2. **Label collision.** No two labels may overlap when the layout settles on
115	   the 128-node fixture. Today, `forceCollide(48)` only protects circles,
116	   so this is currently violated whenever two non-leaf nodes settle within
117	   ~250 px horizontally — a near-certain occurrence on the tree backbone.
118	3. **Settle time.** Time-to-stable layout on the 128-node fixture must be
119	   **≤ 2.0 s** (60% reduction from the ~5.0 s baseline). Achievable by
120	   raising `alphaDecay` to ~0.05, dropping ticks to ~120, or by switching to
121	   a deterministic layout (dagre/elk) that does not iterate.
122	4. **Hub legibility.** The two highest in-degree nodes (degree ≥ 6) must
123	   remain non-overlapping with their neighbours. Today the `-600` charge
124	   spreads them adequately *only* because the graph is sparse; any new
125	   layout must explicitly cover the hub case.
126	5. **Determinism.** Re-mounting the graph with the same data must produce
127	   the same final positions (today it is non-deterministic — `d3-force`
128	   randomly initialises positions on every rebuild). Story 3.B should seed
129	   or replace the layout to make screenshot diffs stable.
130	
131	## Limitations
132	
133	- Label widths use a per-character approximation, not browser
134	  `getBBox`. Cross-checked against the truncation cap (`32 × 6.8 ≈ 218`)
135	  and consistent within ±5 % of native rendering for the default
136	  font stack. Re-measure with `canvas` or a Playwright probe if Story 3.B
137	  needs sub-pixel accuracy.
138	- Wall-clock settle time is compute-only (no `requestAnimationFrame`
139	  pacing). The 60 Hz extrapolation (`300 × 16.67 ms`) is a lower bound for
140	  real-device behaviour.
141	- The fixture is synthetic but shape-matched to the real `docGraph` query;
142	  swap in a snapshot of `library/`-derived nodes if a perfectly faithful
143	  baseline is required.
144

input.old_string:
const freshSimNodes: SimNode[] = currentNodes.map((n) => ({ ...n }))
			const freshNodeById = new Map(freshSimNodes.map((n) => [n.id, n]))
			const freshSimLinks = currentLinks
				.filter((l) => freshNodeById.has(l.source) && freshNodeById.has(l.target))
				.map((l) => ({ source: l.source, target: l.target }))

			simulation = d3
				.forceSimulation(freshSimNodes)
				.force(
					'link',
					d3
						.forceLink<SimNode, (typeof freshSimLinks)[0]>(freshSimLinks)
						.id((d) => d.id)
						.distance(160)
						.strength(0.4)
				)
				.force('charge', d3.forceManyBody().strength(-600))
				.force('center', d3.forceCenter(width / 2, height / 2))
				.force('collide', d3.forceCollide(48))

input.command:
git add src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte e2e/graph.spec.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(web): label-aware collision and bounded convergence in doc-graph [ddx-a9edd569]

Replace forceCollide(48) circle-only collision in D3Graph.svelte with a
custom quadtree force that treats each node as an axis-aligned bounding
box covering the r=18 circle AND the trailing label rendered at x=24,
truncated at 32 chars + ellipsis. Position-correcting (not velocity-only)
so post-settle overlaps stay below 0.5 px.

Tune forceManyBody and forceLink by per-node degree so high in/out-degree
hubs (n0, n1, n7, n14, n21 in the 128-node fixture) get more breathing
room than tree leaves. Raise alphaDecay to 0.05 for bounded convergence
(~120 ticks, ~2 s in the browser instead of the 5 s baseline) and freeze
node positions on simulation 'end' so the graph stops consuming rAF
ticks once stable. Drag-end keeps the codex-recommended fx/fy release
semantics.

TC-043 mounts a 128-node ternary-tree fixture, waits for the new
data-settle-ms attribute, and asserts: no two circles overlap, no label
bbox overlaps a foreign circle, settle ms reported (not gated, per AC4).
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -15
